title: Faulty LCA (Lowest Common Ancestor) using Recursion and Tree Traversal

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree and two nodes `u` and `v`, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of `u` and `v`. The LCA of two nodes is the node with the smallest value that is in the least common ancestor path of `u` and `v`.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a binary tree and a list of node pairs `[u, v]`
statement.notes: The binary tree is a standard binary tree where each node has a value and two children, left and right.

examples.explanation: The LCA of nodes 0, 3 and 5 is 5
The LCA of nodes 1 and 2 is 1

hints.text: Use recursion to traverse the tree and find the LCA

functionSignature.cpp: int lowestCommonAncestor(TreeNode* root, TreeNode* p, TreeNode* q);
functionSignature.java: int lowestCommonAncestor(TreeNode root, TreeNode p, TreeNode q);
functionSignature.python: def lowestCommonAncestor(self, root, p, q) -> int:
